infobox.value: Joshua Carter Jackson   (1978-06-11) June 11, 1978 (age 47)  [Vancouver, British Columbia](/wiki/Vancouver,_British_Columbia "Vancouver, British Columbia"), Canada

(born June 11, 1978)[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a Canadian and American actor. He is known for his portrayals of [Pacey Witter](/wiki/Pacey_Witter "Pacey Witter") on [The WB](/wiki/The_WB "The WB")'s teen drama *[Dawson's Creek](/wiki/Dawson%27s_Creek "Dawson's Creek")* (1998–2003), [Peter Bishop](/wiki/Peter_Bishop "Peter Bishop") in the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* (2008–2013), a troubled married man on [Showtime](/wiki/Showtime_(TV_network) "Showtime (TV network)")'s *[The Affair](/wiki/The_Affair_(TV_series) "The Affair (TV series)")* (2014–2018), and [Christopher Duntsch](/wiki/Christopher_Duntsch "Christopher Duntsch") in the [Peacock](/wiki/Peacock_(streaming_service) "Peacock (streaming service)") crime drama series *[Dr. Death](/wiki/Dr._Death_(2021_TV_series) "Dr. Death (2021 TV series)")* (2021–2023). For the last of these, he was nominated for a [Critics' Choice Television Award for Best Actor](/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Television_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Movie/Miniseries "Critics' Choice Television Award for Best Actor in a Movie/Miniseries"). His other credits include *[When They See Us](/wiki/When_They_See_Us "When They See Us")* (2019), *[Little Fires Everywhere](/wiki/Little_Fires_Everywhere_(miniseries) "Little Fires Everywhere (miniseries)")* (2020), and *[Doctor Odyssey](/wiki/Doctor_Odyssey "Doctor Odyssey")* (2024–2025).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian and American actor (born 1978)

For other people with similar names, see [Josh Jackson](/wiki/Josh_Jackson_(disambiguation) "Josh Jackson (disambiguation)").

**Joshua Carter Jackson** (born June 11, 1978)[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a Canadian and American actor. He is known for his portrayals of [Pacey Witter](/wiki/Pacey_Witter "Pacey Witter") on [The WB](/wiki/The_WB "The WB")'s teen drama *[Dawson's Creek](/wiki/Dawson%27s_Creek "Dawson's Creek")* (1998–2003), [Peter Bishop](/wiki/Peter_Bishop "Peter Bishop") in the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* (2008–2013), a troubled married man on [Showtime](/wiki/Showtime_(TV_network) "Showtime (TV network)")'s *[The Affair](/wiki/The_Affair_(TV_series) "The Affair (TV series)")* (2014–2018), and [Christopher Duntsch](/wiki/Christopher_Duntsch "Christopher Duntsch") in the [Peacock](/wiki/Peacock_(streaming_service) "Peacock (streaming service)") crime drama series *[Dr. Death](/wiki/Dr._Death_(2021_TV_series) "Dr. Death (2021 TV series)")* (2021–2023). For the last of these, he was nominated for a [Critics' Choice Television Award for Best Actor](/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Television_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Movie/Miniseries "Critics' Choice Television Award for Best Actor in a Movie/Miniseries"). His other credits include *[When They See Us](/wiki/When_They_See_Us "When They See Us")* (2019), *[Little Fires Everywhere](/wiki/Little_Fires_Everywhere_(miniseries) "Little Fires Everywhere (miniseries)")* (2020), and *[Doctor Odyssey](/wiki/Doctor_Odyssey "Doctor Odyssey")* (2024–2025).

Outside of television, he came to prominence with his role in the [*Mighty Ducks* film series](/wiki/The_Mighty_Ducks "The Mighty Ducks") (1992–1996). His other film appearances include *[Apt Pupil](/wiki/Apt_Pupil_(film) "Apt Pupil (film)")* (1998), *[Urban Legend](/wiki/Urban_Legend_(film) "Urban Legend (film)")* (1998), *[Cruel Intentions](/wiki/Cruel_Intentions "Cruel Intentions")* (1999), *[The Skulls](/wiki/The_Skulls_(film) "The Skulls (film)")* (2000), *[Gossip](/wiki/Gossip_(2000_American_film) "Gossip (2000 American film)")* (2000), *[The Safety of Objects](/wiki/The_Safety_of_Objects "The Safety of Objects")* (2001), *[The Laramie Project](/wiki/The_Laramie_Project_(film) "The Laramie Project (film)")* (2002), *[Cursed](/wiki/Cursed_(2005_film) "Cursed (2005 film)")* (2005), *[Bobby](/wiki/Bobby_(2006_film) "Bobby (2006 film)")* (2006), and *[Shutter](/wiki/Shutter_(2008_film) "Shutter (2008 film)")* (2008). For his performance in the Canadian independent drama *[One Week](/wiki/One_Week_(2008_film) "One Week (2008 film)")* (2008), Jackson won the [Genie Award for Best Actor](/wiki/Canadian_Screen_Award_for_Best_Actor "Canadian Screen Award for Best Actor").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joshua_Jackson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Jackson was born on June 11, 1978, in [Vancouver](/wiki/Vancouver "Vancouver"), [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") to parents John and Fiona. His mother is a casting director. Jackson's father is from Texas,[[3]](#cite_note-texas-3) and his mother is a native of [Ballyfermot](/wiki/Ballyfermot "Ballyfermot"), a surburb of [Dublin, Ireland](/wiki/Dublin,_Ireland "Dublin, Ireland"). She immigrated to North America in the late 1960s. He has a younger sister, Aisleagh, and two older half brothers, Jonathan and Lyman.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He was raised Roman Catholic.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Jackson lived in California until the age of 8 when he moved to Vancouver with his mother and younger sister; he has said that his father was a bad parent and husband and abandoned the family when they were in the U.S. He attended [Ideal Mini School](/wiki/Ideal_Mini_School "Ideal Mini School") and later switched to [Kitsilano Secondary School](/wiki/Kitsilano_Secondary_School "Kitsilano Secondary School"). He attended high school with actor [Ryan Reynolds](/wiki/Ryan_Reynolds "Ryan Reynolds").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joshua_Jackson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7f/Joshua_Jackson_at_the_premiere_of_Bobby%2C_Toronto_Film_Festival_2006.jpg/250px-Joshua_Jackson_at_the_premiere_of_Bobby%2C_Toronto_Film_Festival_2006.jpg)](/wiki/File:Joshua_Jackson_at_the_premiere_of_Bobby,_Toronto_Film_Festival_2006.jpg)

Jackson at the premiere of *[Bobby](/wiki/Bobby_(2006_film) "Bobby (2006 film)")*, [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival") in 2006

Jackson started acting in a small role in the film *Crooked Hearts* in 1991. The next year, he played the role of Charlie in a musical version of *Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory*. At this point, with the help of the play's casting director Laura Kennedy, he joined the William Morris Agency.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Soon after, he landed the role of Charlie (#96) in *[The Mighty Ducks](/wiki/The_Mighty_Ducks_(movies) "The Mighty Ducks (movies)")* series, playing a young and aspiring hockey player.

Jackson went on to appear as Pacey Witter on *[Dawson's Creek](/wiki/Dawson%27s_Creek "Dawson's Creek")*, which was created by [Kevin Williamson](/wiki/Kevin_Williamson_(screenwriter) "Kevin Williamson (screenwriter)") and ran on the WB network from 1998 to 2003, and also starred [James Van Der Beek](/wiki/James_Van_Der_Beek "James Van Der Beek"), [Michelle Williams](/wiki/Michelle_Williams_(actress) "Michelle Williams (actress)"), and [Katie Holmes](/wiki/Katie_Holmes "Katie Holmes").[[9]](#cite_note-9) While the show was on hiatus, he appeared in several movies including *[Cruel Intentions](/wiki/Cruel_Intentions "Cruel Intentions")* (an adaptation of *Les Liaisons dangereuses* that also starred [Sarah Michelle Gellar](/wiki/Sarah_Michelle_Gellar "Sarah Michelle Gellar") and [Ryan Phillippe](/wiki/Ryan_Phillippe "Ryan Phillippe")), *[The Skulls](/wiki/The_Skulls_(film) "The Skulls (film)")*, *[The Safety of Objects](/wiki/The_Safety_of_Objects "The Safety of Objects")*, *[The Laramie Project](/wiki/The_Laramie_Project_(film) "The Laramie Project (film)")* and a short cameo in the remake of *[Ocean's Eleven](/wiki/Ocean%27s_Eleven_(2001_film) "Ocean's Eleven (2001 film)")* in which he appears as himself in a poker scene with Brad Pitt, George Clooney and Holly Marie Combs. In 2000, he also guest-starred in Season 12 of *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")*, voicing the character of Jesse Grass, a "hunky environmentalist" and love interest for [Lisa Simpson](/wiki/Lisa_Simpson "Lisa Simpson") in the episode "[Lisa the Tree Hugger](/wiki/Lisa_the_Tree_Hugger "Lisa the Tree Hugger")".[[10]](#cite_note-10) He also was cast as "Beau" in the movie *[Gossip](/wiki/Gossip_(2000_American_film) "Gossip (2000 American film)")* in 2000 with actors James Marsden, Kate Hudson and Norman Reedus.

Shortly after *Dawson's Creek* ended in 2003, Jackson played the lead role in films alongside [Dennis Hopper](/wiki/Dennis_Hopper "Dennis Hopper") (*[Americano](/wiki/Americano_(2005_film) "Americano (2005 film)")*), [Harvey Keitel](/wiki/Harvey_Keitel "Harvey Keitel") (*[Shadows in the Sun](/wiki/Shadows_in_the_Sun_(2005_film) "Shadows in the Sun (2005 film)")*), and [Donald Sutherland](/wiki/Donald_Sutherland "Donald Sutherland") (*[Aurora Borealis](/wiki/Aurora_Borealis_(film) "Aurora Borealis (film)")*). In 2005, Jackson moved to the UK and made his stage debut on the [London West End](/wiki/London_West_End "London West End") with [Patrick Stewart](/wiki/Patrick_Stewart "Patrick Stewart") in [David Mamet](/wiki/David_Mamet "David Mamet")'s two-man play, *[A Life in the Theatre](/wiki/A_Life_in_the_Theatre "A Life in the Theatre")*. The play was a critical and popular success, and ran from February to April of that year. Jackson said that he would consider returning to the stage, to try his hand on Broadway. His next film role was in *[Bobby](/wiki/Bobby_(2006_film) "Bobby (2006 film)")*, directed by [Emilio Estevez](/wiki/Emilio_Estevez "Emilio Estevez"), Jackson's co-star from *[The Mighty Ducks](/wiki/The_Mighty_Ducks_(movies) "The Mighty Ducks (movies)")*. He played a lead role in *[Shutter](/wiki/Shutter_(2008_film) "Shutter (2008 film)")*, a U.S. remake of [film of the same name](/wiki/Shutter_(2004_film) "Shutter (2004 film)"). He starred and acted as executive producer in the Canadian independent film *[One Week](/wiki/One_Week_(2008_film) "One Week (2008 film)")*, which opened on March 6, 2009.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/Joshua_Jackson_2012.jpg/250px-Joshua_Jackson_2012.jpg)](/wiki/File:Joshua_Jackson_2012.jpg)

Jackson at the 2012 [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con")

From 2008 to 2013, Jackson played the lead role of [Peter Bishop](/wiki/Peter_Bishop "Peter Bishop") in the science-fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*,[[13]](#cite_note-13) created by [J. J. Abrams](/wiki/J._J._Abrams "J. J. Abrams"), [Roberto Orci](/wiki/Roberto_Orci "Roberto Orci") and [Alex Kurtzman](/wiki/Alex_Kurtzman "Alex Kurtzman").[[14]](#cite_note-14) The series was the second-highest rated new show of the 2008–2009 season after *[The Mentalist](/wiki/The_Mentalist "The Mentalist")*. BuddyTV ranked him #9 on its "TV's 100 Sexiest Men of 2010" list,[[15]](#cite_note-15) #19 in 2011[[16]](#cite_note-16) and #14 in 2012.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Jackson was nominated for a [Genie Award for Best Performance by an Actor in a Leading Role](/wiki/Genie_Award_for_Best_Performance_by_an_Actor_in_a_Leading_Role "Genie Award for Best Performance by an Actor in a Leading Role") for the film *[One Week](/wiki/One_Week_(2008_film) "One Week (2008 film)")*.[[18]](#cite_note-18) He won the award on April 12, 2010.[[19]](#cite_note-19) He held and hosted the satirical Pacey-Con in 2010, directly across the street from the [Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con_International "San Diego Comic-Con International"), sporting a bowling shirt and giving out [fan fiction](/wiki/Fan_fiction "Fan fiction"), written by *Dawson's Creek* fans, to those waiting in the Comic-Con entrance line. Footage of the event was recorded for a video, entitled "Pacey-Con", which he was filming for [Will Ferrell](/wiki/Will_Ferrell "Will Ferrell")'s [Funny or Die](/wiki/Funny_or_Die "Funny or Die") celebrity humor website.[[20]](#cite_note-20) In 2013, Jackson appeared in the IFC film *[Inescapable](/wiki/Inescapable_(2013_film) "Inescapable (2013 film)")* with [Marisa Tomei](/wiki/Marisa_Tomei "Marisa Tomei") and [Alexander Siddig](/wiki/Alexander_Siddig "Alexander Siddig").[[21]](#cite_note-21) Jackson wrote the first story from the comic book trilogy *Beyond the Fringe*, titled "Peter and the Machine".[[22]](#cite_note-22) Jackson starred in the successful television show *[The Affair](/wiki/The_Affair_(TV_series) "The Affair (TV series)")*, where he played Cole Lockhart, the protagonist husband of the unfaithful Alison Lockhart.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

In March 2018, Jackson made his theatrical debut on Broadway in *[Children of a Lesser God](/wiki/Children_of_a_Lesser_God_(play) "Children of a Lesser God (play)")*, where he played James Leeds, an unconventional teacher at a school for the deaf who gets in a conflicted professional and romantic relationship with a deaf former student, Sarah Norman ([Lauren Ridloff](/wiki/Lauren_Ridloff "Lauren Ridloff")). The play ran through May 2018.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

In 2019, Jackson starred as defense attorney Mickey Joseph in the miniseries *[When They See Us](/wiki/When_They_See_Us "When They See Us")*.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

In 2020, Jackson co-starred with [Reese Witherspoon](/wiki/Reese_Witherspoon "Reese Witherspoon") and [Kerry Washington](/wiki/Kerry_Washington "Kerry Washington") in the miniseries [*Little Fires Everywhere*](/wiki/Little_Fires_Everywhere_(miniseries) "Little Fires Everywhere (miniseries)") based on the novel by [Celeste Ng](/wiki/Celeste_Ng "Celeste Ng").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Jackson was cast as Dr. [Christopher Duntsch](/wiki/Christopher_Duntsch "Christopher Duntsch"), a neurosurgeon who was convicted of intentionally maiming his patient, in [*Dr. Death*](/wiki/Dr._Death_(2021_TV_series) "Dr. Death (2021 TV series)") which was based on the podcast of the same name, replacing [Jamie Dornan](/wiki/Jamie_Dornan "Jamie Dornan").[[27]](#cite_note-27)

In 2023, Jackson played the lead in the series *[Fatal Attraction](/wiki/Fatal_Attraction_(2023_TV_series) "Fatal Attraction (2023 TV series)")* with Lizzy Caplan, inspired by the 1980s thriller film of the same name.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joshua_Jackson&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Jackson was in a relationship with *[Dawson's Creek](/wiki/Dawson%27s_Creek "Dawson's Creek")* co-star [Katie Holmes](/wiki/Katie_Holmes "Katie Holmes") during the first two seasons of the show's run. Holmes said that Jackson was her first love.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

From 2006 to 2016, he was in a relationship with German actress [Diane Kruger](/wiki/Diane_Kruger "Diane Kruger").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Jackson began a relationship with actress [Jodie Turner-Smith](/wiki/Jodie_Turner-Smith "Jodie Turner-Smith") in 2018. They married on August 18, 2019.
On April 13, 2020, the couple welcomed their daughter.[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32) In October 2023, it was revealed that Turner-Smith had filed for divorce from Jackson.[[33]](#cite_note-33) In May 2025, it was reported that the couple had reached a divorce settlement.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In December 2023, Jackson and fellow actor [Lupita Nyong'o](/wiki/Lupita_Nyong%27o "Lupita Nyong'o") confirmed they were dating.[[35]](#cite_note-35) In October 2024, Nyong'o announced they were no longer together.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Jackson owned his childhood home in [Topanga, California](/wiki/Topanga,_California "Topanga, California").[[37]](#cite_note-37)
In 2025, the house burned down during the [Palisades Fire](/wiki/Palisades_Fire "Palisades Fire").[[38]](#cite_note-38) He previously lived in [Wilmington, North Carolina](/wiki/Wilmington,_North_Carolina "Wilmington, North Carolina"), where *Dawson's Creek* was filmed, and in New York City, where *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* filmed its first season. In 2009, he moved back to Vancouver to shoot four seasons of the show before the last episode was aired on January 18, 2013.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Jackson is a fan of the [Vancouver Canucks](/wiki/Vancouver_Canucks "Vancouver Canucks") ice hockey team[[39]](#cite_note-39)
and the [San Francisco 49ers](/wiki/San_Francisco_49ers "San Francisco 49ers") American football team.[[40]](#cite_note-40)
